learningObjectives.title: Understand SAP TM integration scope
learningObjectives.description: Explain why SAP TM integrates with SAP and external systems for end-to-end logistics.

learningObjectives.title: Understand integration landscape
learningObjectives.description: Describe major connected systems and business document exchange patterns.

learningObjectives.title: Understand integration benefits
learningObjectives.description: Clarify automation, visibility, consistency, and financial integration outcomes.

sections.content: SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM) does not operate as a standalone application. It integrates with multiple SAP and third-party systems to support end-to-end logistics processes. Integration enables automatic exchange of master data, business documents, execution events and financial information across the enterprise.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Integration allows SAP TM to receive transportation demand from business systems and send execution and financial information back to connected applications.

sections.title: Integration Partners
rows: Sales orders, deliveries and master data

sections.content: A customer order is created in SAP S/4HANA. The outbound delivery is transferred automatically to SAP TM, where Freight Units and Freight Orders are generated. Warehouse activities are managed in SAP EWM, shipment events are received from GPS devices and settlement information is posted back to SAP Finance.

sections.title: Standalone SAP TM vs Integrated SAP TM
Best Practice
sections.content: Use standard SAP integration APIs and business objects wherever possible instead of custom interfaces. This simplifies upgrades and reduces maintenance effort.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A global manufacturing company integrated SAP TM with SAP S/4HANA, SAP EWM and GPS providers. Transportation planning, warehouse execution, shipment tracking and financial settlement became fully automated across multiple countries.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Know the major SAP TM integration points: SAP S/4HANA, SAP EWM, SAP Finance, SAP Event Management, carrier systems and GPS/telematics.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: SAP TM is not designed to function in isolation for enterprise logistics. Most production implementations rely heavily on integration with ERP, warehouse, finance and external logistics systems.

items: SAP TM integrates with multiple SAP and third-party applications.
SAP S/4HANA provides business documents and master data.
SAP Finance handles accounting and carrier payment.
Integration enables complete end-to-end logistics management.
